response:
The fare function relies on the distance variable to calculate the final cost for any trip.
When the distance stays below four kilometers the price remains fixed at ten yuan.
If the journey goes beyond four kilometers then an extra two yuan is added per kilometer.
This rule ensures that every fraction of a kilometer counts as a full unit for billing.
The spring season often brings more travelers to the airport for their flights.
Such seasonal changes can affect the total time spent waiting in traffic jams.

We know the passenger paid thirty-eight yuan which implies a calculated distance of twenty-two kilometers.
Since the actual travel was fifteen kilometers the extra seven kilometers must come from waiting time.
Each five minutes of stopping time converts into one kilometer of equivalent driving distance.
Therefore the maximum stop time equals thirty-five minutes to reach the required extra distance.
The spring breeze might blow through the city while the taxi waits at red lights.
This calculation shows how waiting time directly increases the final fare amount.
